title: chrysanthemum vulgare subsp. boreale
intro: Chrysanthemum vulgare subsp. boreale, also known as Korean chrysanthemum, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the daisy family native to East Asia. This plant is known for its medicinal properties, particularly in traditional Chinese medicine, where it is valued for its anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antiviral properties. The health benefits of Chrysanthemum vulgare subsp. boreale include reducing fever, relieving headaches and respiratory issues, and acting as an antioxidant to protect against oxidative stress. Chrysanthemum vulgare subsp. boreale is often prepared as a tea, infusion, or dried flower for medicinal purposes, and can also be used as a topical application for skin issues and wounds.
